metadata.title: SYNGO_MF_1983
metadata.comment:
Rationale: This paper investigates the motorneuron-Renshaw cell (MN-RC) synapse, a cholinergic synapse in the spinal cord.

Fig.1+2: Sensitivity to DHβE of nicotinic EPSCs in Renshaw cells of various knockout mice are investigated. The most striking changes in DHβE-sensitivity were obtained for mice in which either the β2 or the β4 subunit had been deleted.. Experimental description: -Tissues are of mouse species: "The mice used were either wild type C57BL/6J mice (Janvier), or knockout lines for one of the following nAChR subunits: α7-/-[20], α2-/-[21][22], β2-/-[23], β4-/-[24] or for the membrane anchor of acetylcholinesterase (PRiMA-/- mice, [25])"
